summary: Completed comprehensive frontend testing of BiciFastness static web application. All three HTML pages load correctly with proper branding, navigation, and form structures. Critical Firebase Firestore index configuration issue identified that prevents ranking data from loading.

critical_bugs.issue: Missing composite index for tiempos_viaje collection queries
critical_bugs.impact: Ranking page cannot load any data - shows error message
critical_bugs.error_details: FirebaseError: The query requires an index. Console URL provided in error message.
critical_bugs.affected_functionality: All ranking queries with where() and orderBy() clauses

integration_issues.issue: Cannot display ranking data due to Firebase index missing
integration_issues.status: Shows proper error message to user

passed_tests: index.html loads with BiciFastness branding and correct tagline
All three pages have consistent navigation between them
Route filter dropdown present with correct options (Todas las rutas, Legazpi - Casa, Méndez Álvaro - Casa)
Ranking table structure present with all required columns
subir.html form has all required fields: usuario, ruta, minutos, segundos, foto
File upload area is functional with drag-and-drop support
admin.html login form present with email/password fields
Firebase SDK imports are correct (modular v10.7.1)
CSS styling uses correct BiciMAD green color (#00A651)
Navigation between all pages works correctly
Login form shows proper error handling for invalid credentials
All pages are responsive and display correctly
action_item_for_main_agent: CRITICAL: Create Firebase Firestore composite indexes for the tiempos_viaje collection. The specific index URL is provided in the console error. Without this index, the ranking functionality cannot work. All other frontend functionality is working correctly.
